pairs.question: Who founded the International Save the Children Union (UISE) and in what year?
pairs.answer: The International Save the Children Union (UISE) was founded in 1920 by Eglantyne Jebb and her sister Dorothy Buxton.

pairs.question: What was the primary goal of the International Save the Children Union (UISE) when it was established?
pairs.answer: The primary goal of the UISE was to create a powerful international organisation that would extend its influence to the remotest corners of the globe to address child welfare.

pairs.question: Which two prominent members of the International Committee of the Red Cross were involved in the UISE, and in what capacity?
pairs.answer: The UISE included Etienne Clouzot, who was the Head of Secretariat of the International Committee of the Red Cross, and at least one other prominent member.

pairs.question: What significant child rights document did the UISE help establish in 1924?
pairs.answer: The UISE lobbied for and agreed to the Declaration of the Rights of the Child, which was adopted by the League of Nations in 1924.

pairs.question: How did the International Save the Children Union (UISE) evolve in 1946?
pairs.answer: In 1946, the UISE merged with the International Association for the Promotion of Child Welfare (founded in 1921) to form the International Union for Child Welfare.

pairs.question: What organisation was formed in 1977 to coordinate international advocacy work among Save the Children organisations?
pairs.answer: In 1977, the International Save the Children Alliance (renamed Save the Children International in 1997) was formed to coordinate international advocacy work.

pairs.question: Why did the General Council of the International Union for Child Welfare vote to disband the organisation in 1986?
pairs.answer: The General Council voted to disband the International Union for Child Welfare in 1986, allegedly due to financial mismanagement.
